question: Provide a breakdown of average monthly household spending and total household counts, categorized by social network contact types and religious or caste groups, sorted from highest to lowest expenditure.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.CASTE_RELIGION_GROUP_8_CATEGORY, T2.SOCIAL_NETWORK_CONTACT_2_TYPE, AVG(T1.MONTHLY_TOTAL_CONSUMPTION_EXPENDITURE_RS), COUNT(T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CONSUMPTION AS T1 INNER JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_SOCIAL AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID LEFT JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD AS T3 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID GROUP BY T2.CASTE_RELIGION_GROUP_8_CATEGORY, T2.SOCIAL_NETWORK_CONTACT_2_TYPE HAVING COUNT(T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID) > 0 ORDER BY AVG(T1.MONTHLY_TOTAL_CONSUMPTION_EXPENDITURE_RS) DESC

    ds.SOCIAL_NETWORK_CONTACT_2_TYPE,
    ds.CASTE_RELIGION_GROUP_8_CATEGORY,

question: Identify the states and district codes where households either spend more than 60,000 rupees monthly or employ five animal care workers.

predicted_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT gh.STATE, gh.DISTRICT_CODE
FROM DIM_HOUSEHOLD h
JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CONSUMPTION fc ON h.HOUSEHOLD_ID = fc.HOUSEHOLD_ID
JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY gh ON fc.GEOGRAPHY_ID = gh.GEOGRAPHY_ID
WHERE h.HOUSEHOLD_NUMBER_OF_ANIMAL_CARE_WORKERS = 5 
   OR fc.MONTHLY_TOTAL_CONSUMPTION_EXPENDITURE_RS > 60000;

question: Identify the codes for rural districts where at least one household has a monthly total expenditure below 500 rupees, while another has a non-food expenditure exceeding 2,000 rupees.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.DISTRICT_CODE FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CONSUMPTION AS T1 JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID WHERE T2.CENSUS_2001_URBAN_RURAL = 'Rural' GROUP BY T2.DISTRICT_CODE HAVING MIN(T1.MONTHLY_TOTAL_CONSUMPTION_EXPENDITURE_RS) < 500 AND MAX(T1.MONTHLY_NON_FOOD_EXPENDITURE_RS) > 2000
predicted_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT gh.DISTRICT_CODE
FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CONSUMPTION fhc
JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY gh ON fhc.GEOGRAPHY_ID = gh.GEOGRAPHY_ID
WHERE gh.CENSUS_2001_URBAN_RURAL = 'Rural'
AND gh.DISTRICT_CODE IN (
    SELECT DISTRICT_CODE FROM (
        SELECT gh.DISTRICT_CODE,
            COUNT(CASE WHEN fhc.MONTHLY_TOTAL_CONSUMPTION_EXPENDITURE_RS < 500 THEN 1 END) AS low_expenditure_count,
            COUNT(CASE WHEN fhc.MONTHLY_NON_FOOD_EXPENDITURE_RS > 2000 THEN 1 END) AS high_nonfood_count
        FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CONSUMPTION fhc
        JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY gh ON fhc.GEOGRAPHY_ID = gh.GEOGRAPHY_ID
        WHERE gh.CENSUS_2001_URBAN_RURAL = 'Rural'
        GROUP BY gh.DISTRICT_CODE
    ) sub
    WHERE low_expenditure_count > 0 AND high_nonfood_count > 0
);
raw_output:
SELECT DISTINCT gh.DISTRICT_CODE
FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CONSUMPTION fhc
JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY gh ON fhc.GEOGRAPHY_ID = gh.GEOGRAPHY_ID
WHERE gh.CENSUS_2001_URBAN_RURAL = 'Rural'
AND gh.DISTRICT_CODE IN (
    SELECT DISTRICT_CODE FROM (
        SELECT gh.DISTRICT_CODE,
            COUNT(CASE WHEN fhc.MONTHLY_TOTAL_CONSUMPTION_EXPENDITURE_RS < 500 THEN 1 END) AS low_expenditure_count,
            COUNT(CASE WHEN fhc.MONTHLY_NON_FOOD_EXPENDITURE_RS > 2000 THEN 1 END) AS high_nonfood_count
        FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CONSUMPTION fhc
        JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY gh ON fhc.GEOGRAPHY_ID = gh.GEOGRAPHY_ID
        WHERE gh.CENSUS_2001_URBAN_RURAL = 'Rural'
        GROUP BY gh.DISTRICT_CODE
    ) sub
    WHERE low_expenditure_count > 0 AND high_nonfood_count > 0
);
